sections.title: Talking to them for the first time

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Oh look, a human!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: A human? Can we eat it?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: No, you rock brain! Humans are our friends!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, excuse me...

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Not sure if I would call them our friends... Not enemies either, of course.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Not like the trolls. We hate trolls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me, I just want to-

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Nothing's worse than a troll.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Except discovering that your pickaxe's gone blunt just as you-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me!!!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Why, hello, yes, what is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I er... well, who are you?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: I'm Derni.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: I'm Dernu.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: And I'm Miodvetnir. These are my brothers. And who might you be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My name is [player name].

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Pleased to meet you, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: I say, we don't get to see a hummon down here very often!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: It's a human, you metal head! You should pay more attention!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, can I ask you dwarves a few questions?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Of course, we love answering questions!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: We're very good at answering questions!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Fire away!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Are you lost down here?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Or were you just stopping by for a chat?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just stopping by for a chat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's down this river?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Oh, you don't know?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: [He/She] doesn't know?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: [He/She] doesn't know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't you just tell me?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: It is our glorious city!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: The grand city of the dwarves!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Sparkling jewel of the mountain!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Keldagrim, our home!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you take me there?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Oh no, we can't do that.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: We're far too busy.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: You have to keep busy, you see.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Always keep busy, is what our good father used to say.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Besides, there's been some weird going ons in the city.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Don't tell [him/her] that! None of [his/her] business!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Only fair if [he/she] wants to go into the city.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Oh yes, they only just cleaned up the mess from the last little incident.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: So watch out if you ever do manage to travel to the city! Weird things could happen!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any good spots to fish around here?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Fish?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Fish?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: What are fish?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: No fish around here.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Say, you're the Fishing Champion, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: That must be why you were talking about fish then...

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: What are fish?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: But this water here, you see, it comes dripping down from above.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Molten snow, you see.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: What are fish?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Oh, they're these small wriggly things that live in the water.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: But they're not all small! Some can be large!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: And they don't breathe air.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: But [player name] could tell you all about it! After all, [he's/she's] the Fishing Champion!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe later...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are these mines used for?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: What does it look like they're used for?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Isn't it obvious?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: For mining of course!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: These mines are actually the territory of one of the eight major companies of the mining Consortium.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mining companies? Where are they at then?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Oh, they all have their headquarters in the city.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: First floor of the palace.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: No need to give [him/her] directions.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: We haul all our goods to the city by ship, you see.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Since the mine carts don't come here.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: But because the river goes right to the city, this is just as easy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with this rock down in the mines?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems to be unbreakable. I need to know why.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Say, is that Dondakan's rock?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: That big unbreakable rock?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: That's what [he/she] said, isn't? Pay more attention!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, yes, that's the one.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Oh, you're not going to get through there I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: It's unbreakable, you see.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Dondakan's been there for...

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: How long would you say he's been there, Miotvednir?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Eighteen years, Derni, and not a year shorter!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: That's some dedication!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: But look at what it got him.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Nothing, that's what.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What material would break that rock? You know, the impenetrable one?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Well... have you tried a rune pickaxe?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: An adamantite tool?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: What about a bronze pickaxe?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: A bronze pickaxe??

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: What good would that do?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Well, you never know...

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Don't you think that's the first thing they tried?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Hey, it was just a suggestion!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anyone named Rolad? Some kind of a dwarven scholar?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Sorry, doesn't ring a bell.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Oh yes, I know Rolad!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Oh, stop winding up the human, Dernu!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where he is right now?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: No idea, why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dondakan wants to shoot me out of a cannon!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Oh dear!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: How awful!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: That's terrible!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He thinks I'm actually going to fly through it and land on the other side.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: That sounds a bit daft.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: How would that work?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: I think you're going to hurt your head.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Old Dondakan might have finally lost it.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: He was always obsessed with that rock.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: And gold.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Right, and gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any tips on how to defeat an evil demon?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: An evil demon, you say?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: I'd run away if I ever ran into an evil demon.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Don't be such a coward, Dernu!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Well, what would you do then?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: I don't know, I've never encountered an evil demon.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: I once met a good demon though.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Oh yes, what did he say?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: He gave me a puzzle box and then went off again.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: I think you must have had too much dwarven stout in you.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Could be, could be, but there's no telling, is there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I defeated the spirit of the rock!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: That's amazing!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: That's fantastic!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: What rock was that again?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Pay attention for once, Dernu! It's Dondakan's rock!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: That's quite impressive, Player. How did you manage to do that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you see, I just-

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: What kind of question is that to ask? With [his/her/their] great might of course!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Or vast magical skills!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: No, you're more a ranger, aren't you Player?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I-

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Anyway, this calls for a celebration.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Oh yes, we should go down into Keldagrim to celebrate.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Tonight, of course, not now. We're on duty now.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Right... and I'm sure traffic here will pick up soon with that rock broken.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: I heard there's vast amount of gold inside!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: The company is going to be rich!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Now don't you go and discuss company business in front of this human, Dernu.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Right. Let's get back to work.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Anything else you wanted, Player?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help with this statue... King Alvis' statue, to be precise.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Oh yes, what's the problem then?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: It was knocked down, remember? Parts of it nearly landed on our heads?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: A terrible noise it made too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This sculptor has got me looking for a lot of items, you see.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Items eh?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: We love items.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Can't get enough of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, can you he-

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: I got this puzzle box once, you know.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Oh, not that story again.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: You have told that story before, Dernu.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Well forget about it then. I'll shut up if you don't want to hear about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, the sculptor only needs to build the head now. But I need to get the Consortium to help me with that.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: The Consortium, you say?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: You're going to talk to the Consortium?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: My, you're certainly moving up in the dwarven world, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: What do you need us for then?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: We are but simple dwarves.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Especially you, Dernu.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Now now, Derni, there was no need for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have rebuilt the statue for the [company name]!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: What, really?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Of course, really! Player wouldn't have said it otherwise, would [he/she/they]?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: And didn't you see Blasidar working on the statue?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Oh yes, he was out there for quite a while.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: That must have been a huge task.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Nothing's too difficult for old Blasidar.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: He truly is a master builder.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: We can all take an example to his hard work and dedication.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Speaking of, shouldn't we get back to work ourselves?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Quite right. Anything else you wanted, Player?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know about this drunken dwarf...?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Drunken dwarf?

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Yeah, we know a drunken dwarf!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: In fact we know plenty!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: There's this one really annoying dwarf though, he sometimes throws rocks at me for no reason.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, tell me about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one I mean is his relative, living in the northeast of Keldagrim. I wonder if you could help me with him.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Ah yes, him.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Such a sad case, poor fellow.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Who is he?

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Don't you know anything?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Now now, there's no reason to get angry.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: I suppose you're right. Well, he's a drunk dwarf that talks about kebabs a lot.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Has some pretty odd tales to tell too.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: And I understand he's the relative of that dwarf I mentioned earlier.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: I hope that helped, Player?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this unforgettable tale to tell you!

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: A tale!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: We love tales!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: We live for tales!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Like, there was this one time when I lost my spanner in the mines...

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Player wants to tell a tale!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: But my spanner... it's a very interesting tale you know.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: I think we've heard it before.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: It was pretty forgettable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me, do you want to hear my tale or not?

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: Happy to.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Would love to hear it.

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: As long as it's better than my spanner story.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: So, what's your tale like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I errrr...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have forgotten.

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Must have been pretty forgettable as well then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must be off now, thanks for the chat.

lines.speaker: Miodvetnir
lines.text: No problem!

lines.speaker: Dernu
lines.text: Glad to be of service!

lines.speaker: Derni
lines.text: Come back any time!
